sources.title: CCR T14 §7.50(b)(70) — Kings River (Fresno Co.)
sources.quotedText: Kings River (Fresno Co.). (A) Kings River, South Fork from its trout confluence with Copper Creek downstream to the Highway 180 crossing at Boyden Cave. All year. 2 trout. 4 trout in possession. (B) Kings River South Fork, from the Highway 180 crossing at Boyden Cave downstream to the main stem; Middle Fork, from the western boundary of Kings Canyon National Park downstream to the main stem; and main stem, from the confluence of the South and Middle forks downstream to Garnet Dike Campground. All year. Only artificial lures with barbless hooks may be used. 0 trout (C) Kings River, from Garnet Dike Campground downstream to Pine Flat Lake. All year. 2 trout. 4 trout in possession. (D) Kings River from Pine Flat Dam downstream to U. S. Army Corps of Engineers Bridge on Pine Flat Road. Closed to all fishing all year. (E) All year. 5 trout 1. Kings River from the U.S. Army Corps of Engineers Bridge on Pine Flat Road downstream to Cobles (Alta) Weir. 2. Kings River Thorburn Spawning Channel, the 2,200-foot-long channel located 5 miles downstream from Pine Flat Dam, and the reach of river within a 200-foot radius of the channel exit. Closed to all fishing all year. (F) Kings River, from Cobbles (Alta) Weir downstream to the Highway 180 crossing. All year. Only artificial lures with barbless hooks may be used. 0 trout (G) Kings River from the Highway 180 crossing downstream. All year. 5 trout

sources.title: CCR T14 §7.50(a)(4) — General possession limit provision for listed trout waters
sources.quotedText: Unless otherwise provided, it is unlawful to possess more than one daily bag limit.

sources.title: Kings River Fisheries Management Program — Thorburn Spawning Channel
The Ecological Angler — Lower Kings River
California Fish and Game Code §7145 — Sport Fishing License Requirement
sources.quotedText: Except as otherwise provided in this article, every person 16 years of age or older who takes any fish, reptile, or amphibian for any purpose other than profit shall first obtain a valid license for that purpose...

groups.humanSummary: Kings River (Fresno Co.) is divided into seven lettered reaches from the South Fork's Copper Creek confluence down through the San Joaquin Valley, alternating between general trout water (2-5 trout), artificial-lures/barbless-hooks catch-and-release water (0 trout), and two year-round closures (the reach immediately below Pine Flat Dam, and the Thorburn Spawning Channel).
groups.verbatimText: Kings River (Fresno Co.). (A) Kings River, South Fork from its trout confluence with Copper Creek downstream to the Highway 180 crossing at Boyden Cave. All year. 2 trout. 4 trout in possession. (B) Kings River South Fork, from the Highway 180 crossing at Boyden Cave downstream to the main stem; Middle Fork, from the western boundary of Kings Canyon National Park downstream to the main stem; and main stem, from the confluence of the South and Middle forks downstream to Garnet Dike Campground. All year. Only artificial lures with barbless hooks may be used. 0 trout (C) Kings River, from Garnet Dike Campground downstream to Pine Flat Lake. All year. 2 trout. 4 trout in possession. (D) Kings River from Pine Flat Dam downstream to U. S. Army Corps of Engineers Bridge on Pine Flat Road. Closed to all fishing all year. (E) All year. 5 trout 1. Kings River from the U.S. Army Corps of Engineers Bridge on Pine Flat Road downstream to Cobles (Alta) Weir. 2. Kings River Thorburn Spawning Channel, the 2,200-foot-long channel located 5 miles downstream from Pine Flat Dam, and the reach of river within a 200-foot radius of the channel exit. Closed to all fishing all year. (F) Kings River, from Cobbles (Alta) Weir downstream to the Highway 180 crossing. All year. Only artificial lures with barbless hooks may be used. 0 trout (G) Kings River from the Highway 180 crossing downstream. All year. 5 trout

regulations.humanSummary: Open all year, South Fork Kings River from the Copper Creek confluence downstream to the Highway 180 crossing at Boyden Cave
regulations.verbatimText: Kings River, South Fork from its trout confluence with Copper Creek downstream to the Highway 180 crossing at Boyden Cave. All year.

parameters.counting_rule: 2 trout per day; 4 trout in possession (as expressly stated, overriding the general 1x-daily-bag possession default).

regulations.humanSummary: 2 trout daily bag, 4 trout in possession, South Fork above Boyden Cave
Open all year, catch-and-release, South Fork below Boyden Cave / Middle Fork below the Kings Canyon NP boundary / main stem to Garnet Dike Campground
Only artificial lures with barbless hooks may be used
regulations.verbatimText: Only artificial lures with barbless hooks may be used.

regulations.humanSummary: Open all year, Garnet Dike Campground downstream to Pine Flat Lake
regulations.verbatimText: Kings River, from Garnet Dike Campground downstream to Pine Flat Lake. All year.

parameters.counting_rule: 2 trout per day; 4 trout in possession (as expressly stated, overriding the general 1x-daily-bag possession default).

regulations.humanSummary: 2 trout daily bag, 4 trout in possession, Garnet Dike Campground to Pine Flat Lake

parameters.note: Kings River from Pine Flat Dam downstream to the U.S. Army Corps of Engineers Bridge on Pine Flat Road is closed to all fishing all year.

regulations.humanSummary: Closed to all fishing all year, immediately below Pine Flat Dam
regulations.verbatimText: Kings River from Pine Flat Dam downstream to U. S. Army Corps of Engineers Bridge on Pine Flat Road. Closed to all fishing all year.

regulations.humanSummary: Open all year, Corps of Engineers Bridge on Pine Flat Road downstream to Cobbles (Alta) Weir

parameters.counting_rule: 5 trout daily bag limit; possession limit equals one daily bag limit (5) per the general provision at 14 CCR §7.50(a)(4) absent a separately stated possession limit.

regulations.humanSummary: 5 trout daily bag limit, Corps of Engineers Bridge on Pine Flat Road downstream to Cobbles (Alta) Weir
regulations.verbatimText: Kings River from the U.S. Army Corps of Engineers Bridge on Pine Flat Road downstream to Cobles (Alta) Weir. All year. 5 trout

parameters.note: Kings River Thorburn Spawning Channel (a 2,200-foot-long channel about 5 miles downstream of Pine Flat Dam) and the reach of river within a 200-foot radius of the channel exit are closed to all fishing all year.

regulations.humanSummary: Closed to all fishing all year, Thorburn Spawning Channel and the 200-foot radius around its exit
regulations.verbatimText: Kings River Thorburn Spawning Channel, the 2,200-foot-long channel located 5 miles downstream from Pine Flat Dam, and the reach of river within a 200-foot radius of the channel exit. Closed to all fishing all year.

regulations.humanSummary: Open all year, catch-and-release, Cobbles (Alta) Weir downstream to the Highway 180 crossing
regulations.verbatimText: Kings River, from Cobbles (Alta) Weir downstream to the Highway 180 crossing. All year.

regulations.humanSummary: Only artificial lures with barbless hooks may be used
regulations.verbatimText: Only artificial lures with barbless hooks may be used.

regulations.humanSummary: 0 trout, catch-and-release only, Cobbles (Alta) Weir to the Highway 180 crossing
Open all year, below the Highway 180 crossing
regulations.verbatimText: Kings River from the Highway 180 crossing downstream. All year.

parameters.counting_rule: 5 trout daily bag limit; possession limit equals one daily bag limit (5) per the general provision at 14 CCR §7.50(a)(4) absent a separately stated possession limit.

regulations.humanSummary: 5 trout daily bag limit, below the Highway 180 crossing
regulations.verbatimText: Kings River from the Highway 180 crossing downstream. All year. 5 trout

parameters.note: Anglers 16 years of age or older must have a valid California sport fishing license.

regulations.humanSummary: California sport fishing license required for anglers 16+
regulations.verbatimText: Except as otherwise provided in this article, every person 16 years of age or older who takes any fish, reptile, or amphibian for any purpose other than profit shall first obtain a valid license for that purpose...
